payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Exactly. To get you guys up to speed  the party, Vox Machina, after having a relatively violent encounter with a couple known as the Briarwoods-- Lady Delilah Briarwood and Lord Sylas Briarwood, two individuals who were responsible for essentially torturing and murdering Percy's family and taking over his town of Whitestone and for the past five years have ruled it, guiding it into some dark direction they've begun to recently unravel. The party traveled to Whitestone to clear their name as they were essentially put in a bad political position with their home town of Emon and to possibly bring vengeance for what Percy's family had undergone five years before. Upon arriving they found the town in a very dark emotional place. Zombie giants roving the streets as these controlled centurions. A number of individuals in the town feeling the general unrest. And they decided to build a revolt. Percy helming the de Rolo crest, Vox Machina went around inciting the thoughts of rebellion in the city. Not the first but apparently the one with the most chance of succeeding they've had to this date. After some infiltrations and sizable victories cutting down some of the underlings of the Briarwoods within the city, the people began to arm themselves, rise up, and fire, blade and screaming took the city as the denizens began to fight back. In this chaos, Vox Machina made their way underneath the Castle Whitestone, where the Lord and Lady Briarwood currently reside, seeking some sort of a project called the ziggurat they've been working on for a while. While they were down there they found a woman, apparently imprisoned, under an illusion that they revealed to be Anna Ripley. Dr. Anna Ripley, one of the torturers of Percy five years before and currently seemingly interested in the black powder based gun tech he had been developing. However, they took her captive, went upstairs, and discovered that Percy's sister, Cassandra, is indeed still alive and being held captive within the castle. In the process of trying to save her Anna Ripley got away, Cassandra got her throat cut but thankfully was saved by the quick actions of Vox Machina and the near death's door stepping of Vax once again. But nevertheless, saved her, prevented her disastrous fate--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, is it sold out again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Certain sizes are.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Certain sizes are?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We're pushing to get more in, hopefully.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Better get them while you can.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And the Critmas sweater, which is delightful, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It is delightful, isn't it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You can also get these by donating to the Obama campaign eight years ago. Just so you know.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know if they have any left but they might.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Does anybody else have any announcements they want to make at this time?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am nervous.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I am so nervous.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And terrified.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm calm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Perfect, perfect. Well, cool. That being the case, then, let's go ahead and let's go ahead and jump in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit, okay.

payload.raw_text: Exactly. preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And the Critmas sweater, which is delightful, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

preceding_turns.text: Does anybody else have any announcements they want to make at this time?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am nervous.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I am so nervous.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And terrified.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm calm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Perfect, perfect. Well, cool. That being the case, then, let's go ahead and let's go ahead and jump in.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit, okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So, having Cassandra saved, some intense conversations began. Some discussions of some elements of the Briarwoods' real intentions. They found notes within Professor Anders's room, one of the other individuals responsible for rising up against Percy's family back in the day. Found materials that could clear their name back in Emon and Cassandra armed up. They made their way down to the basement of the city into the undercroft and through the mausoleum that contained the descendants of the de Rolo family. After which, a series of ghosts, seemingly haunting that vicinity since the darkness that took this kingdom took hold, began to possess the party into a rough battle in which they were forced to fight themselves. The ghosts were ousted, destroyed, and the party, regathering what powers they still have at their disposal to begin to delve into the seemingly recently dug tunnel beneath the city of Whitestone towards whatever this ziggurat project is. And that is where we left off last time. As you guys have taken a moment to gather your breath after the undercroft infiltration of these spirits, one of the last of which Scanlan shamed into a redeath-- (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Before anyone entered the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's true. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You've all gathered yourself, taken a short rest to heal up. Whatever else you want to do to prepare at this time. You know, discussion, items, preparation, trading, anything you want to do before you delve any further into this tunnel underneath the city of Whitestone, now is the time to do so.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: If you wanted to attune to something during that short rest, you can.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes. I consider the matter settled.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know exactly what was going to happen, but I saw a blade against your sister's throat. I would hope you would do the same for my sister if you were to see the same thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course he would.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: But mostly because I fear her.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan, back me up. If you were to turn into some sort of three-horned beast and go running into a house full of enemies, we would give him a similar talking-to, correct?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That would never happen. I'm more measured than all of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We don't actually believe that he will listen to sense.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm Percy. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We just can't waste our breath, really.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan, shut up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Attunement. How long does that take?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is going to be a very complicated evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Should we take a short rest?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We just did.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We did?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, we did!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Of course. It is true I have been living here, at least in this castle with them, for the past three years. Not, not of choice, of course. And were I in your position, would think the same."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Right, that was a stupid suggestion, Keyleth.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, come on, we've got to stay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're all Seemed differently; it's very strange.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm really disappointed, Keyleth.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Cassandra, I want to say something a little frank. I'm very happy that we've found you. I'm very happy that Percival and you have been reunited. We have been burned very badly in the past by seeming allies. And I am sure you are in your right mind. But past experience has taught us to be very careful. And we are all watching you. For signs of corruption or control. I want to believe you are in control of your faculties. I do not fully trust you are and I think, just letting you know that a knife could hit your back as soon as the slightest misstep happened, is maybe enough to ensure some safety for this group.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well, I was near death. I was recovered by the father that runs the temple of Pelor and a few other individuals who had fled the city at the time. I was as far-- as I was concerned, I was gone. But they nursed me back to health. Slowly. Over time. I was, for the better part, useless as an individual for about six months. And even to this day, I have a bit of a wheeze when I expend myself, but Father Reynal made sure that I was in good health. And that was when I learned truly what had become of the city in my restoration. And it was there that we began to speak of the early talks of rebellion, here in Whitestone. Me being still alive was enough of a beacon, for those who knew, for us to put together our first attempt to fight out from under their rule. And it was marked. They knew every step we took. Many lives were lost, but in that fray, they recognized me. They didn't know I was alive, either. I guess they saw the importance of keeping me under their lock and key as a way of crushing whatever hope those who still lived here had."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course. It is true I have been living here, at least in this castle with them, for the past three years. Not, not of choice, of course. And were I in your position, would think the same."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: According to Percival, they filled you with arrows. What happened?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "That would have certainly made a statement. There were many days when they first took me captive I would have welcomed that. But in that time we planned that first rebellion, I saw fire in the eyes of my people. Brighter than any I had witnessed in my youth. I saw their love for my family. I saw the intent of these people to take back what was theirs from this corrupt force, and I knew that I had a part to play. If they choose to leave me alive, as much as they try and sway my interests, it also brought me close to them. I watched them. I could see what they were planning. Try and gather details of this construct they're working on beneath the city and feed this information to those who I knew were still true and behind the de Rolo family name. I don't know what I can say to offer you more than that."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Why not hang you from that tree in the center of town like they did so many others?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. You guys continue on this slowly descending tunnel for about ten minutes or so before you see it divide to the left very heavily and then continue forward. There's still no light source here and so you're relying on your darkvision to continue, but there's the switchback to the left and the one that continues forward.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can, it'll be like throwing a t-shirt over your bedroom light. It'll just be a nice atmospheric Chinese lantern.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, mood lighting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I place my Sarenrae hand on her shoulder and say, you're here because she willed you to be here, she knows that we need you. Now please hide behind obese Percival. And I pat my sister on the shoulder and head back into the darkness.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. So as Pike comes to the back, smiling at everybody, she joins the back ranks of Keyleth and Trinket. Actually, probably behind Percy. Cassandra's going to be right there. Flying V formation. And you guys continue down this tunnel.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Check for traps.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Perception all that shit down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, perception. Oh yeah, I should do it too. Hold on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 20 for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I know, right? 18 for me.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 16, okay. You get down and inspect the area. You actually pull out a small bit of torch pith and light it a bit just to get a little light across this one intersection briefly. There are footprints back and forth in all ways, but many of them have been, at least the footprints coming from, and the last footprints were coming from this tunnel to your immediate left, were from a while back. The fresher footprints, fresh as of four hours, continue on down that way. You do notice that the smell of the air itself is, you notice this as well as you're inspecting, the air down here has a very acrid, sharp scent anyway, a hint of chemical. It seems to be getting stronger to the left.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 20 for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I know, right? 18 for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You guys continue on this slowly descending tunnel for about ten minutes or so before you see it divide to the left very heavily and then continue forward. There's still no light source here and so you're relying on your darkvision to continue, but there's the switchback to the left and the one that continues forward.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I lean over to my sister and say, I'm not going to do anything, I'm just going to look 50 feet down--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How about wait a fucking second and let me look for footprints?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I look for footprints?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can. Make a survival check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I don't know which one to use. I'm so nervous. Okay, let's do this one. That was a bad one. I'm perceptioning?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is a survival.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Well, it's the same. That is a 16.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. Continuing to push forward, about another five minutes or so, keeping track of your distance to the castle you're starting to get towards the outside perimeter of the actual city itself. As you're keeping tabs on distance traveled to that area, you catch, based on your last perception roll, there is a portion of the floor that seems to have some placed crooked chunks of stone. You see pieces of broken stone here and there, things that have fallen out from being hauled from the distant dig into wherever the ziggurat is, but there's one section where it appears there's platforms.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Well, it's the same. That is a 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16, okay. You get down and inspect the area. You actually pull out a small bit of torch pith and light it a bit just to get a little light across this one intersection briefly. There are footprints back and forth in all ways, but many of them have been, at least the footprints coming from, and the last footprints were coming from this tunnel to your immediate left, were from a while back. The fresher footprints, fresh as of four hours, continue on down that way. You do notice that the smell of the air itself is, you notice this as well as you're inspecting, the air down here has a very acrid, sharp scent anyway, a hint of chemical. It seems to be getting stronger to the left.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's where the acid pits are. Acid. Briarwoods. Briarwoods?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You continue forward for another I'd say 20 minutes. Go ahead and make another perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that thieves' tools?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's thieves' tools, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's a 27.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You find two of the large stone slabs that are part of this discarded sequence here, they have beneath them some small magical burst that is waiting to detonate. You manage to mark it and take a couple of other stone pieces to wedge underneath it to prevent it from depressing onto whatever the trigger may be, and note to the rest of the party to avoid that section.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: There's a couple of stones here that I've marked in the middle of the path. Don't step on them, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Copy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Somebody tell Trinket.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He fucking understands signs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll be in charge of guiding Trinket.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He listens to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The awkward large bear-Percy--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They got the message.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's still my friend. At least the bear still loves me.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. It seems to be pervasive for now. It may eventually fade or sink into the earth, but it'll take a while for that to happen. But you move past this trap. You continue forward and the descent grows steadily more and more steep at this point. What was like a relative decline has now gotten to the point where some of you have to get up against the wall as you step down, not to the point where it's slide-worthy or anything, but enough where it gets a little precarious for those who don't have quite as much a grand hold on loose earth. About five minutes of this, it evens out though, and as it does you come to a metallic doorway.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You have advantage on persuasion checks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You take half poison damage whenever you do take poison damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, where did Pike go?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No Pike! The evil of this place is making it hard to maintain her connection.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I know it's hard. It's deep underground.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hey!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: This astral projection is very hard to hold. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We live in an age of miracles and wonders.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So as you guys pass under this cloud, you--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: The connection is just not very strong.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's all right, it's much better now. Keyleth, you push the toxin cloud back and hit it off to the side, pushing it down the same tunnel that the acid section was. It'll take you a while to get it back there, but you can just leave it lingering in the tunnel if you'd like.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, it's fine. I'll just leave it. It's fine.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No, actually. Most of this tunnel structure did not exist when you lived here. This has all been either re-discovered or created since you left Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So, can we clear out and I'll try to shoot it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: See what happens.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'll do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So all of you step back out into the hallway, your cluster of Percys and Cassandras.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I step one foot behind Cassandra Number Two.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Go ahead and pull your arrow. You aim carefully from just outside the doorway.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You've got this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do I add my attack to it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you add your attack bonus.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 27. So you release the arrow. Perfect hit. On point. It strikes whatever this strange object is embedded in the floor. It hits and slightly ricochets and splinters as an arrow would when it hits a hard surface. No seeming reaction.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It did nothing!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to try something. First of all, does the construct of this at all remind me of anything?

payload.raw_text: Okay. You make your way up this side looking about on the floor. Nothing seems to happen, nothing catches your eye. You get onto this upper platform, and there is the door there in front of you.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Leaning my back against the wall.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay. They pass overhead. As you make your way underneath the three opals in this part of the wall. Making your way up the other ramp to the other side.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. That's seven. So three on one side, three on the other, Trinket keeping there with anticipation up next to you. Pike, you put your hand on the final stone. A moment passes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I walk over to the wall and touch one of the other gems.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I touch another one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I touch another one!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys all place your hands against the opals on the sides, and checking as you pull your hand away, the opals glow brightly white again, and then fade.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I am Captain Planet. No.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Touch one of the stones, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I run over and touch a stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I touch one, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) All right, so that's three on one side, three on the other, who's got the middle one? You got the middle one?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm in the middle.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I got one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Trinket.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, can Trinket touch a stone? Or Cassandra. Cassandra, make yourself useful for Christ's sake.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Who's Christ?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: My friend back in Gnomolia, where I'm from.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike, are you grabbing a stone or not?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What was that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Are you touching a stone or not?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I am.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The Feast gives you advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go ahead and make your first shot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's 15. 27.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 27. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To hit. And that's ten plus three points of ice damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. The bullet slams into the bottom of this tube, and the tube itself is not made of any real thick material; it's a similar material to the bronze the rest of this room is constructed in, and it seems like it's brought in by some sort of mechanism that locks into place. As the bullet strikes the bottom of it, it bends upward, closing off part of its passageway, and locking off the other half with ice, seemingly holding it for the time being. While you're reloading, I need you to go ahead and make a wisdom saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I have advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What about these jewels?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That was for the damage? Oh, got you. So as you slam into it and slam into it, with each progressive hit, you can see the glass itself is gliding off the blade. You're doing bits of damage, but it's going to take you a while. You could get through this in about ten minutes, but right now the acid has lifted to the point where the next round, everyone's going to start being submerged.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But he's Percy-sized right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pretty sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well, it's fake, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Let me double-check this real fast. Yeah, a gnome's considered small, so it would have to be a small creature, for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I can take Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Just try it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: There's four of us in here, and four gems left.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How many gems are left inside?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Four. But it could be that that's the only gem that's necessary.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Or I could go outside the stone door and see if there's any more buttons out there to press.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think going outside is a good idea. Go outside.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Grog, do you want to make your strikes?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yes, let's go!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Go.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The best thing I've ever heard. You and me in a room alone together.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: First two hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, it was 15, 20, and 23 on the hits. That was the damage.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You can't quite move the metal enough this time. It's still arcing, and it's spattering against the glass. No effect on the glass, but it's starting to hit elements of the ceiling. You can see the stone is being eaten away. At this point, everyone who's currently on the ground suffers three points of acid damage, as it's now beginning to reach up to your shins. You take two.

preceding_turns.text: Okay. You guys run up and put your hands on the gems.

preceding_turns.text: They've both gotten two gems each, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.text: Come on, work, damn it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You could, yeah. You could probably get one gem. There's only four gems left.

preceding_turns.text: Could Trinket do the other one? Where's Trinket?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Trinket is up against the wall on the sides, very scared right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We need a fourth hand!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay! Still keeping my Heat Metal spell up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You place all of your hands on the gems? No apparent effect.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I pull out my sword and start whacking it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make another strength check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shitballs. We're going to die.

preceding_turns.text: Guys, the Briarwoods' personal quarters are really plush. They're sick.

preceding_turns.text: (laughs) I rolled a zero!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You rolled zero again? (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: But you do arc it over a little further, and it's now (splatter noise) pouring onto the ceiling and splashing over and down over the glass wall. You can see, the stonework is melting through and after a few seconds of this, you begin to see the top of where the glass wall ends.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.text: So roll two d20s, take the higher of the two, and add your spell attack modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Cold as ice, Mercer.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.text: We're hitting gems, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.text: I'm hitting my gem, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Roll damage.

preceding_turns.text: Oh no.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

payload.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.text: I'm flying.

preceding_turns.text: Very uncomfortable for everyone involved.

preceding_turns.text: Which, when Trinket reaches the top, it's like trying to fit an oblong object through a hole.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm flying so I get underneath him and try and wedge him, help him get into the hole.

payload.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.text: It's holy arrows. All of them will be holy arrows, because Conjure Barrage uses whatever kind of ammunition I have shot.

preceding_turns.text: That it does. (cheering)

preceding_turns.text: So what's the saving throw on that?

preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's a good question. Conjure Barrage is-- dexterity save.

preceding_turns.text: We're going to grave. There we go. Proper Briarwood music.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I believe Uncanny Dodge, you just roll a saving throw, and if you make it, you take no damage.

preceding_turns.text: I believe so, yeah.

preceding_turns.text: Okay. Oh, that's it? I just do that? 3d8, that's it?

preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nine. Not including the d4, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not including the d4.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So that's four damage to both Sylas and Cassandra. You take no damage from it because of Uncanny Dodge.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I call out and say, Vex'ahlia, what are you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so roll a d4.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Two.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Two. Okay. Two additional points of damage to Sylas. All right, and as the smoke begins to dissipate?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to start running. I'm booking to the right, and I don't have any more actions, do I?

payload.raw_text: You recall that lightning seemed to not have the impact it usually does.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I come around the corner.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you're about here, so you can move forward.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The second I see her, Delilah, I will cast Lightning Bolt on her.

preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.text: Lightning Bolt? She's immune.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know anything.

preceding_turns.text: All right, you see her jump back into the cloud, and you unleash.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Actually, are they in a line? Is there a way I could get in a line?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not with this pillar there, no. This pillar's blocking the way. Right now, you're only seeing her, at this point, since she goes into this fog cloud, so you can throw it in the direction she is, if you want to.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I wouldn't know anything about her, so yeah. I blast.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It was a long time ago.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 12, so it's now ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: 34 hits. The other two do not.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

preceding_turns.text: And then that would be his turn, so two hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Slow him down.

preceding_turns.text: You take 13 points of slashing damage from the first strike, and then you take ten points of slashing damage from the second strike.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's 26 points.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I need you to make two constitution saving throws.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: 23 points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And make two constitution saving throws.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.text: Okay. You feel as each strike of the blade cuts into your flesh, a portion of your body's strength is sapped from it. Your strength goes down by two.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: My strength is a negative one.

preceding_turns.text: No, I mean your score. Not the modifier, your score.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You can. So you rush up, around the pillar, clicking your Sprinter's Boots to give the distance, you rush up into the cloud, right into the face of Vax. And Vax, you look down at Pike, now aggressively coming towards you. Protecting Lady Briarwood, you pull your daggers out instinctively and look down at her with a look of "What are you doing?" You reach forward and reach out your hands against him. Let me check Restoration real fast. Let me see here, Greater Restoration. 5th level. Ends effects like charmed or petrified. As you reach out and touch him you feel the impact of the Briarwoods' influence fade from your mind. As she touches you, the charm is dispelled and you look at your daggers and look where you are and look at the moments and the last few minutes and you're like--

preceding_turns.text: 60 foot?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.text: Oh god. Just know Ashley, that sprinting in like this will take you right in front of the Briarwoods.

preceding_turns.text: Oh, because I can't see the board.

preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's not a good thing. Maybe it's better to--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are near Vax, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I get right behind him and cast Greater Restoration on him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can. The fog obscures them; they have three quarters cover. You can still see their shapes in the cloud, it's not completely obscured.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And there's a pillar between you and me in the middle of a 30-foot stretch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So you can hide behind it after you cast it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I try that?

payload.raw_text: There's no saving throw. If you have less than 150 hit points, you are stunned for the next minute. You have to make a saving throw every turn to resist it.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You glance around the side for three-quarters cover. Roll a perception check.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's a dead roll, a dead roll, it's between two. That's terrible. Four.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah, you do not see this happening, unfortunately. Your mind suddenly goes blank as the Power Word that's spoken suddenly stuns you in place, unable to react.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. That ends your turn. It is now Sylas's turn. Sylas, looking over this way. He does not regenerate any hit points, because of the multitude of holy and radiant damage he took last turn, his regeneration is stunted at the moment. He has to keep away from Keyleth. He is going to, instead... he's in a rough place. Then he's going to move one, two, three, four, five, six... Nope! Actually, he's going to-- you know what? He's going to take it. He's going to go ahead and boldly step into the sunlight, which puts him at disadvantage with his attacks. But he is going to unload on you, Percy, who he marked previously.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Same thing, 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And a 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 14 misses.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24 damage, on the first one?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: 24 for the first one. And 22 for the second one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: But she doesn't take any holy damage off of the oil on the thing, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, but as the hammer slams into her armor she gets slammed against the pillar from the impact of it. She tries to right herself and push off against the wall. You use the momentum to spin around and hit her in the other side, slamming her up against the wall on the side of the structure and you hear her breath escape and she goes (gasping breath). Is having to catch her breath. And she looks at you with this look of "That really hurt!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I've been known to take a lady's breath away! (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You want to move, or will you stay where you are?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll stay where I am.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He rolled a 19 and a 20. My rolls for him have been pretty good these past few rounds. So that's another 13 points of slashing damage against you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You would be able to get right about level this turn, and next turn you could go beneath. So you would not be able to hide as an additional this turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, then I want to stay high. And I'm going to use my bonus action to cast Hunter's Mark on her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh man! I should have done that at the beginning, but I thought I was going to hide again. What a waste.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. So that ends your turn. Percy, you're up. Actually, at the end of your turn, Sylas is going to use another one of his legendary actions to make another attack on Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: God-fucking-damn it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How many of those fuckers does he have?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he gets three every turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He gets three every turn?!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Every round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: After every round, I mean. That will be-- he has disadvantage, so he doesn't crit. (laughs) But that is still a 30.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, so that's the end of Grog's turn? All right. It is now Sylas's turn. Sylas is going to step off to the side here, stepping off to the stairs to get out of the range of the sunlight. Just outside of the radius of it. And, glancing over at you, after you just unleashed that swing of attacks towards his wife, he goes, "You. Help us." Make a wisdom saving throw with advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I will go on her. I don't like her, so I'll punch her in the face.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay! Goes forward. Go ahead and roll for your spell attack against her.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay. Is this through the cone?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There isn't a cone aspect to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, so do I just roll to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Here you go, I'll pull it up right here. All right, this is the clenched fist? You make a melee spell attack. Roll a d20 and add your spell attack bonus.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. (giggles) Spell attack. 16?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16. As the fist (whoosh) towards her, she ducks and it slams into the pillar, cracking the stone slightly.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shit. How far away am I from both of them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From both of them? You are about 45 feet from Sylas and about 65 feet from her.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I get ten feet closer to her.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (quietly) Can you inspire Vax?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: This is the rest of my turn. I already used it to inspire Grog.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All righty. So, with that, it brings us to the top of the initiative round. It is now Lady Delilah Briarwood's turn. Looking around, heaving with anger and frustration, she looks around her area and the tears streak down her face, blood streaking down one side of her head. She looks at Cassandra, who right now is faltering. Seeing everything closing around her, she goes, "It's too soon, but it's our only chance." And with that, she is going to step around here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: May I ask a question?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Does Cassandra's charm come from Sylas or Lady Briarwood?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, you have no idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What about yours?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: From Lady Briarwood, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The lady.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The lady.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yours is fine. All right, does that end your turn, Keyleth?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, so a little time goes by and then eventually it wears off.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I float down in front of him and slap him across the face.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Like that? Okay. Make another saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You slap, slap. He's taking it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I go over to Vax?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Cassandra's still up, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cassandra is.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to Remove Curse on him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You cast Remove Curse. Let me see. This is not considered a curse, so as the spell alleviates its energy into Vax, he still stands there, staring into space.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh god.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, wait, are we slapping Vax? Let me try.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. You said something about a minute before, though, didn't you?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. Bigby's Hand does last a minute, if you want to keep it maintained. The minute has passed at this time. It's probably going to pass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You can handcuff her behind her back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We do have manacles.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Put her in the Bag of Holding.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL - SAM - MATT
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe Greater Restoration her.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, can I Greater Restoration on her?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you have another level of that spell?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then you cannot. It's a 5th-level spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can save it for later.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I take some of the manacles, and I manacle her hands behind her back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What's up, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do you have Greater Restoration?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not on me. Not today.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to start working with that door.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And to be super shitty, I grab a length of rope and wrap it around her ankles and tie it back to her hands so she can't stand up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Sorry, Cassandra. That's going to suck.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: She's got to stay there when she wakes up.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It'll require a short rest to attune and get the nature of it, but it is a very beautiful new weapon.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then you cannot. It's a 5th-level spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We can save it for later.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I take some of the manacles, and I manacle her hands behind her back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What's up, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do you have Greater Restoration?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not on me. Not today.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to start working with that door.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And to be super shitty, I grab a length of rope and wrap it around her ankles and tie it back to her hands so she can't stand up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Sorry, Cassandra. That's going to suck.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: She's got to stay there when she wakes up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Bigby's Hand does last a minute, if you want to keep it maintained. The minute has passed at this time. It's probably going to pass.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, before it passes, it gives me a quick deep-tissue massage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I pick up Sylas's onyx sword?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What does a common healing potion do, by the way?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Those who got 15 or higher, you notice the writhing on the walls. The walls are encased in what looks like a tapestry of bodies. Hundreds of humanoid individuals that are woven into each other, each missing their left hand and/or left eye from their body, and they're all just squirming and writhing. Many of them are in various states of decay, and they're just moving after that ice that was encasing them had shattered. There's a low, weird moaning drone that's beginning to fill the room. The black orb itself is starting to rise out of the center of the pedestal, as well. Now, Vex, since you had the top of this, and Vax. What do you want to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I take a potion really fast?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the top of your turn, you can, as a bonus action.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The orb is floating?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The dark orb in the center of the platform that she's currently on in the center of this room. It's half-embedded in the ground, and she scatters her blood across it. You can see her shaking with fear and trepidation during this whole circumstance.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She splatters her blood on the dark sphere?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check, those who are up top, by the way. Who's up next?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Jesus Christ. 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: One.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What are we trying to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Perception. Scanlan gets up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey. Hi, guys! What's going-- oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's bleeding all over that thing?

payload.raw_text: So as Grog rushes down the steps of the ziggurat, clutching the unconscious and bleeding body of Lady Delilah Briarwood. Vax rushing behind, carrying the barely-conscious, limp body of Vex'ahlia, the rest of you racing out. You, your hand bloodied from the terrible force of whatever that was. You go charging down the steps of the ziggurat. Is anyone taking Cassandra, or are you leaving her behind?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay. As you're holding her in your arms, what's everyone doing?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'm picking up my sister and lurching after Grog.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'm picking you up, and I'm lurching after Grog!
